text.text: The Embodiment of Eternal Finality, The Echo of All-Laws' Return to Void  To wield this blade is not to bear a weapon, but to hold destiny itself. Its edge cuts not through metal or wood, but through the very fabric of existence. When the first breath of annihilation whispers against its sharpness, the threads of causality begin to unravel, and the curtain of reality fades silently.  This artifact bears no inscription, for its mere existence is the most ancient scripture—a paradox of creation carved upon the membrane of dimensions. What flows across its surface is not metallic luster, but captive micro-singularities, within which countless worlds are born and return to nothingness every instant.  Legend speaks of a time before the realms were divided, when the Primordial Deity sought to glimpse eternal truth, only to behold an absolute "Nothingness." In dread, It forged this vision into form—this very blade. Thus, where its edge passes, there is no destruction, only "Return"—the restoration of all things to their primordial state of non-being.  The mundane see but a blade. The wise perceive the "negation-state" of the entire observable cosmos. Those who bear this instrument speak not of conquest, but of granting grace—bestowing upon all existence the most serene, most equal, most absolute final benediction: Annihilation.  "I do not slay gods; I merely grant them slumber." — Last words of the Final Bearer
